item.title: War, oil, growth and inflation
item.description: Plus very very long-term interest rates

item.title: The cynical opportunities of ‘Epic Fury’
item.description: With this US administration, the best bet is that a business deal is to be reached with Iran

item.title: A contentious climate fix gathers pace
item.description: Carbon capture and storage is making unexpected breakthroughs

item.title: When buying clothes, can you beat the market?
item.description: There are more than just financial considerations to take into account when buying vintage, quality basics and luxury items

item.title: European broadcasters need to look to Paramount-style M&A
item.description: As pressure to do a deal grows, buying quality content rather than viewers is the way to go

item.title: Can Reese’s have too many pieces? The line-extension conundrum
item.description: The company has come under fire for its use of ‘chocolate coating’ and ‘peanut butter creme’

item.title: What if AI just makes us work harder?
item.description: Employees have reported increased momentum, but also a feeling of having more to do

item.title: Italy’s media industry is in upheaval
item.description: Legal battle with controversial YouTuber echoes broader tussle

item.title: Less radical, more profitable: here come Europe’s new fintech unicorns
item.description: Business-to-business services are bread and butter for a lot of investors

item.title: Stamp duty is Britain’s quiet growth killer
item.description: The rate of housebuilding has plummeted over decades; to achieve recovery, the government needs to scrap high transaction taxes

item.title: Should I marry my partner to protect me financially?
item.description: He is terminally ill and I’m worried my child and I won’t be able to stay in our home when he is gone

item.title: A dull UK fiscal statement comes with a fragile outlook
item.description: As global risks mount, Rachel Reeves must shore up Britain’s finances and growth agenda

item.title: Macron’s remarkable nuclear proposal faces a culture clash
item.description: The longstanding independence of France’s ‘force de frappe’ may stand in the way of successful co-ordination with allies

item.title: Wanted: private equity buyers for unloved online marketplaces
item.description: Smarter operators in a depressed and AI-disrupted sector can still find an edge

item.title: Iran war presents a different script for markets
item.description: Investors have been right to identify geopolitics as a rising risk factor

item.title: Donald Trump’s dangerous ‘war of whim’
item.description: Anyone who claims to know where this conflict will go is bluffing

item.title: What’s a safe asset now?
item.description: Most of the old standbys are not working

item.title: Britain’s ‘open’ voters need a champion
item.description: Despite their triumph in Gorton, it’s not clear the Greens have what it takes to prevent a Reform victory at the next election

item.title: B&M tries to win back Britain’s discount shoppers
item.description: Retail chain’s new chief executive needs to catch up with rival Home Bargains

item.title: France’s groundbreaking nuclear offer to Europe
item.description: Macron’s decision to extend the deterrent is a boost for the continent’s defence

item.title: AES’s private equity buyers pull off the self-leveraging buyout
item.description: Investors in power and utility group might have expected more but it’s not clear they had a right to

item.title: Ghana’s ageing cocoa farmers are caught in a price trap
item.description: Small farms and low productivity mean their livelihoods did not improve even as markets hit a record high

item.title: Airlines’ healthy finances will be tested by Middle East conflict
item.description: Carriers have enjoyed strong demand and cheap fuel but escalating tensions threaten both

item.title: Iran war is upending gas markets
item.description: The disruption to LNG flows has sparked concerns about supply to the key regional customers of Europe and Asia

item.title: Hormuz crisis is a wake-up call for complacent gas consumers
item.description: The world has already reckoned with its dependence on oil but retains other energy vulnerabilities
